checks.finding: All four required elements present. (1) Metric row named: 'Repeat Finding Rate row / Friction Patterns section of future retrospectives' in ## Verification Metric, confirmed to already exist and be populated in the source retrospective's own 'Comparison With Previous Retrospective' table. (2) Baseline/target stated: 'Baseline: 2 (epic-159-pillar-a, this period: T-001 F-2, T-004 F-3)' / 'Target: 0'. (3) Task-cycle horizon stated: 'within the next 2 completed features that include a bootstrap/investigation phase'. (4) Threshold stated: numeric Target: 0, reiterated in Expected Effect.

Evidence scale: 2 of 4 tasks in a single feature carried an Accepted Minor finding of this exact class, zero Critical/Major, zero rework cycles (retrospective FP-01 'Do Not Overfit' note characterizes this as contained). Change scope: one targeted rule addition to a single file/section (AGENTS.md Rules) directly naming the root-cause mechanism. Matches the precedent of Verified WFI-001/WFI-002/WFI-003, each adding a single AGENTS.md Rules subsection for comparably scoped evidence. Proportional.

Scanned docs/workflow-improvements/ for Status: Verified WFIs (WFI-001, WFI-002, WFI-003, WFI-005, WFI-006, WFI-007). WFI-001/002/003 target AGENTS.md; verified via grep that AGENTS.md Rules currently contains exactly their subsections ('Evidence report identity fields', 'High-risk task preflight', precheck-fallback text) and no existing rule about citation of factual claims/grep evidence for spec-authored premises. WFI-011's proposed subsection is additive and topically non-overlapping. No overwrite, conflict, or contradiction found.

Root cause is a missing evidentiary-citation expectation during the investigation/requirements-authoring phase, not a script-enforced gate limitation. Mechanism: instructions. Confirmed via grep that task-review-loop and impl-review-loop SKILL.md files already explicitly consume AGENTS.md-declared rules ('per AGENTS.md', provenance re-review rule), demonstrating reviewer agents read and apply AGENTS.md Rules without plugin modification. A project-side rule is plausibly sufficient, consistent with the WFI-001/WFI-003 precedent.

app-dev-efficiency concrete detail intact: feature slug and task IDs named in Root Cause Hypothesis; Proposed Change names the specific file and section with concrete failure examples. No genericization introduced (Cycle 1 applied zero revisions).

Target of 0 (from baseline 2) within the next 2 features is not implausibly optimistic: narrow well-defined finding class (Minor only, zero rework), the change directly targets the identified mechanism, and the horizon is not aggressively short.
Category: app-dev-efficiency does not create a GitHub Issue.

Meta-Change: false is explicitly declared with rationale and verified consistent with the change content: the added rule instructs reviewers during existing qualitative review judgment (instructions mechanism) and modifies no script, threshold, grader, or run-record definition — matching the non-meta classification of the structurally analogous Verified WFI-001 and WFI-003 additions.

summary: WFI-011 passes all eight Cycle 2 impact/risk checks. Verification Metric and Plan jointly specify the retrospective row, baseline/target, task-cycle horizon, and improvement threshold. The proposed AGENTS.md Rules addition is proportional to a contained, single-feature, Minor-only friction pattern and does not conflict with any Verified WFI's prior AGENTS.md changes. The fix is plausibly achievable via project-side instructions alone, confirmed by existing plugin skill references to AGENTS.md-declared rules. Meta-Change: false is correctly declared. Verdict: PASS.
